lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: Rest in bed for a bit?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Some rest never killed anybody.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: I still have stuff to do.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text:
As you walk near the house a man sitting on the porch looks at you.
he looks old and wears camo trousers and a white tank top. You aproach cautiously.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Hey. how is it going young man?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: hello. Fine...who are you?

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text:
Im your new neighbor! Me and my family just moved in last week. 
Figured id get to know the people around here.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Well I'm Jessie. Nice to meet you.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Im Michael. I have a son around your age maybe you could get to know eachother sometime.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: I guess we could.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Hes at school right now. Why arent you as well by the way?

choices.text: Ummm. I have a cold? (Youre lying)

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Ummm. I have a cold? (Youre lying)

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Oh okay. Hope you get better soon then. Bye.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Oh haha. Well try not to get caught then. Adam already got into problems for missing one lesson because of his...

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: *the man shuffles slightly*

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: well..us moving in. Obviously.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Oh that sucks. But dont worry about me. Besides I have some important stuff to do.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Sure. Come by some time in the evening. Adam will be home by then and you can meet.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Sounds good! See you later.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Leave me alone.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Wow. No need to be rude...

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You look at the porch but the neighbor seems to not be at home. Or maybe they dont want to talk to you

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: Its the afternoon this part of the game is work in progress.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text:
You aproach the campfire. You remeber all the memories you had here with friends and family.
 You look behind a particular looking log where you hide your cigarets.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Oh hey. Theyre still there. And olny a little soggy and moldy. Maybe I should take them?

choices.text: Yeah, maybe theyll come in handy.
Nah. Dont need to get caught with them on me.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text:
Its late and too dark to see anything here. 
	Maybe only the scary monsters your father told you about here many years ago.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You grab the cigarets

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You leave the cigarets there. You can return to take them later if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text:
You come back to the campfire theres nothing here but your hiding spot. 
	Do you want to put the cigarets back in the hiding spot?

choices.text: No, ill keep them on me for now.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You keep the cigarets on you. Be careful not to get caught.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You leave the cigarets there. You can return to take them later if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: The flower your mom has been watering instead of you seems like its healthy and growing.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Mom always told me that in times of need i should pluck a single branch from the flower and hold on to it. Superstision i guess...unless?

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: Do you take a branch?

choices.text: Sure, mom knows what shes talking about.
No, its a waste to ruin such a pretty flower.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You leave the flower alone.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: The part of the flower that you have torn the branch from seems to already be recovering.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Neat. The flower isnt dead because of me.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You tear off a branch as carefully as possible.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: Do you want to hide the key in the same spot as before?

choices.text: Yes. Dont need to be caught with them.
No. A bit longer I still have stuff to do.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You make sure to recreate the exact position and rotation that it was originaly in.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Looks about right.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You better make sure not to get caught with it.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You look under the cutting board that suspiciously is always in this exact spot.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You find a key. It looks similar to the one youve seen your dad lock the door to their room with.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Hmmm..

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: The gods favour me today. Better hide that key in the deepest pocket i have.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Parents arent at home. Maybe i could have a look inside?

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You decide to walk away.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Parents always tell me not to go there...what are they hiding?

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You enter the one room your parents always tell you not to enter.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: The doors to your parents bedroom. Mom and dad are at home so better not even look at it.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: The door to parents room is locked

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: What are they hiding? They never allow me in.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: Do you want to leave the room?

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You exit the room.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You decide to stay

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: I still have things to do but i need to be careful.

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: Hey kid! want me to give you a lift to school?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: No ill walk. Its healthier anyways.

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: Since when do you care about cardio?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Since your car didnt start last week and I had to explain to my teacher why im late.

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: Fair enough...

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: It works now though.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Nah Im good.

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: Suit yourself. Just dont be late or Ill throw you to the wolves.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: The wolves would probably be better parrents to me than you two.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Shut up smartass and go get dressed.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Yessir.

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: Go get dressed and dont forget to eat something.

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: Mom coming home in a week so better get used to making your own breakfast.

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: Hey we didnt get to speak in the morning. I would have taken you to school if you asked.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Sorry. I was in a hurry

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: Could have given you a lift to school if you bothered to talk to me.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Yeah like last time? pass..

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: That was one time and it not my fault somebody left the key in the ignition.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Its your car! Nobody else drives it but you!

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: Okay, Okay. Cut me some slack, I am old man.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Okay old man. Try to not debilitate our only mode of transportation while moms away.

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: Sure and you go fry ants with a magnifying glass or whatever you do in your free time.

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: Anyway have you met the new neighbors?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: We have new neighbors?

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: Oh my god you really do live under a rock.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Dude if I live under a rock so do you! WE LIVE IN THE SAME HOUSE!

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Okay that wasnt as funny as I imagined in my head.

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: ...So you planning on visiting them?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Maybe. How long are they in town?

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: A few days. Maybe you could make a new friend.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: I mean-

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: Maybe he wont catch onto how weird you are for couple minutes so you can feel like a human.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Ok thats just mean.

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: Oh So its okay to give ME a hard time for forgetting my keys but its wrong for ME to make fun of YOU?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: It actually is because Im your special only child and you should cherrish me.

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: The day ill start cherrishing you is the day you start providing for this household.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You sigh in annoyance

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Im fourteen.

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: So is the neighbors kid. Go meet him he seems like a good kid.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: I met the new neighbors.

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: And? how was it? Is the boy even weirder than you?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Yes somehow he manages to beat me on that.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: And one thing i noticed. He must have some sort of a condition or something.

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: What sort?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: I dont know. I havent noticed anything. Its just that-

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: His father mentioned something about him missing school for a reason.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: But he didnt finish it so im not sure. He didnt seem like there was something wrong with him.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Well apart from not getting like any of my jokes.

lines.speaker: father
lines.text: Well whatever it is I hope I dont have to tell you to be respectfull and accepting.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Dont worry.
